data.title: IdeaTek Is Bringing Gigabit Fiber Internet to Parsons
data.description:
IdeaTek is investing $8 million to build a 100% fiber network in Parsons, Kansas, delivering speeds up to 8 Gbps to 4,900 homes and businesses. The project aims to enhance connectivity, boost economic growth, and provide reliable, high-speed internet without contracts.

Parsons, Kansas--(Newsfile Corp. - March 28, 2025) - A faster, more connected future is coming to Parsons. IdeaTek is making an $8 million investment to deliver a blazing-fast, 100% fiber network to the city, setting a new standard of connectivity.

IdeaTek Is Bringing Gigabit Fiber Internet To Parsons

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8814/246438_f198778164198b66_002full.jpg

The Buhler-based internet provider will deliver unlimited, uncapped speeds of up to 8 gigabits per second - cutting-edge performance that is far beyond what other providers can offer. The project will give 4,900 homes and businesses the reliability and bandwidth they need for generations to come.

Mayor Verlyn Bolinger applauded IdeaTek's commitment to bring fiber internet to Parsons, calling it a great step toward community growth.

"Access to fast, reliable internet is no longer a luxury—it's a necessity for businesses, schools and families," he said. "IdeaTek's investment in Parsons will boost economic growth, support local businesses and improve everyday life for our residents. We're excited to see the positive impact this will have on our community."

Construction in Parsons is set to begin immediately with the first neighborhoods connecting in May. Residents can learn more about IdeaTek's services and pricing at ideatek.com/parsons-ks.

Unlike some providers that use terms like "Fiber-Powered" to disguise outdated technology, IdeaTek delivers true 100% fiber connections. Fiber offers symmetrical speeds, unmatched reliability, and scalable capacity-all at more affordable pricing than many existing providers.

"At IdeaTek, we're committed to bringing our high-speed fiber internet to every corner of Kansas, and we're excited to make it a reality in Parsons," said Daniel Friesen, IdeaTek's co-founder and chief innovation officer. "True fiber internet is the gold standard in internet connectivity. We believe everyone deserves the best possible online experience, whether you're casually browsing or online gaming, our fiber network delivers unparalleled reliability. IdeaTek's ultimate goal is to provide fiber to all Kansas residents because fiber is hands-down the best technology for high-speed broadband."

Friesen added, "We're so confident you will prefer our service over others that we don't require contracts. We'll provide you with the best service, so we're not worried about you leaving."

Parsons residents can soon enjoy:

  • 100% Fiber Network - A pure fiber network, designed for speed, reliability and future growth.

  • Symmetrical uploads & downloads - Speeds up to 8 gigs - fast speeds only fiber can provide.

  • Unlimited Data - Fiber can carry infinite amounts of data, and IdeaTek won't charge clients for usage.

  • Reliability - IdeaTek fiber delivers a consistent, high-speed connection, even during peak hours-something coax cable can't match.

  • No contracts - Clients stay because they love it, not because they are locked in.

  • Local customer service - Local support from real Kansans.

Community Investment

IdeaTek is driven by its mission of Internet Freedom, working to bring fiber internet to all Kansans-no matter where they live or their circumstances. As the company continues to expand its regional presence in 2025, fiber projects are currently underway in Baxter Springs, Garden City, Winfield, Arkansas City and Wichita, along with other Kansas communities.

Celebrating its 25th year in business in 2024, IdeaTek was founded in 1999 by Friesen and four friends while attending Buhler High School. Today, the company employs more than 180 Kansans and continues to grow. IdeaTek has deployed over 6,500 miles of fiber and built 150 fixed wireless towers, serving customers in over 110 Kansas communities. Learn more at ideatek.com.

https://maps.app.goo.gl/5uJAJFc1dmShjUCg9

About the company: IdeaTek Telcom is a Buhler, Kansas-based broadband service provider with the mission of "fighting for Internet Freedom." IdeaTek uses a unique and innovative approach to deploy scalable, long-term fiber-optic infrastructure, working to bridge the digital divide in Kansas communities. IdeaTek is a champion for broadband equity and is committed to enriching the communities they serve. The company was recently named to Inc. 5000's list of fastest-growing companies for the 10th time.

Contact Info:

Name: Amy Bickel

Email: pr@ghostlylabs.com

Organization: IdeaTek

Address: 111 Old Ml Ln, Buhler, KS 67522

Website: https://ideatek.com

Cannot view this video? Visit:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pP2ZkTGaoug

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/246438

data.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on lululemon athletica to $315 From $375, Keeps Equalweight Rating
data.description: lululemon athletica has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $379.59, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Canggang Railway's Profit Slides in 2024
data.description:
Canggang Railway recorded a decline in attributable profit in 2024 to 56.5 million yuan from 59.0 million yuan in the year-ago period, a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Earnings per share were 0.0147 yuan in the year, down from 0.0152 yuan in 2023.

Revenue for the railway operator fell by 26% to 258.9 million yuan in the year from 349.0 million yuan a year prior, mainly due to a lower revenue from rail freight transportation and ancillary businesses.

The firm recommended a final dividend of 0.006 yuan per share for the year, payable on or around July 9 to shareholders of record on June 27.

data.title: Polystyrene (PS) contract prices march higher in Europe
data.description: This week, PS contract prices climbed, while spot prices remained flat in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "European polystyrene (PS) contract prices saw an increase as the month's settlements were concluded. However, as the month advanced, some negotiations faced delays attributed to falling styrene spot prices, with reports suggesting that final settlements ended up lower than the styrene contract delta. Overall, demand for polystyrene remained low, although the packaging sector demonstrated some resilience during the month. Looking forward, a converter indicated that demand for ice cream packaging is anticipated to grow with the approach of summer. The construction sector continued to face challenges, particularly with weak activity reported in Northwest Europe. Furthermore, purchasing trends among end-users significantly influenced polystyrene consumption. Economic uncertainty has negatively impacted demand for white appliances, which has, in turn, affected the expandable polystyrene (EPS) market, as EPS is frequently utilized in the packaging and transportation of these appliances. Imports into Europe persisted, although a converter mentioned that many of these shipments were ordered two months earlier and had only recently arrived due to shipping delays. Market participants generally foresee stable demand in the upcoming months. However, a converter in Southern Europe pointed out that April may be a shorter trading month due to several holidays in Italy, leading them to prepare for smaller-volume purchases. In the spot markets, GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1565-1575/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, steady week on week, while HIPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1665-1675/mt FD North West Europe levels, stable from last week. EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1740-1750/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, unchanged week on week.In the contract markets, GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1690-1695/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both gaining by Euro (+20/mt) from last week. GPPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1690-1695/mt FD NWE France levels, higher by Euro (+20/mt) from the previous week. Meanwhile, GPPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1415-1420/mt FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+15/mt) week on week. In the contract markets, HIPS prices were assessed at the Euro 1790-1795/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both rising by Euro (+20/mt) from last week. HIPS prices were also assessed at the Euro 1790-1795/mt FD NWE France levels, a week on week gain of Euro (+20/mt). Meanwhile, HIPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1495-1500/m FD NWE UK levels, up GBP (+10/mt) from the previous week.In the contract markets, EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 2200-2205/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both surged by Euro (+60/mt) from last week. EPS prices were assessed at the Euro 2200-2205/mt FD NWE France levels, a steep rise of Euro (+60/mt) from last week. Meanwhile, EPS prices were assessed at the GBP 1840-1845/mt FD NWE UK level, a sharp gain of GBP (+45/mt) from last week.Upstream SM spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 1215-1225/mt FOB Rotterdam levels, up USD (+5/mt) from the previous week.European Styrene contract price for March 2025 has been settled at the Euro 1554/mt CIF North West Europe. This price represents a sharp gain of Euro 65/mt from its February 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: India's SPML Infra rises on plan to enter battery energy storage system sector
data.description:
** Shares of SPML Infra rise 2.8% to 160 rupees

** Infrastructure development company approved entry into battery energy storage system sector

** Trading vol at 186,416 shares, 1.2x the 30-day average

** SPML down ~37% YTD

data.title: CureVac rises on COVID vaccine patent decision
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of German drugmaker CureVac , rise 15.4% to $3.23 premarket

** European Patent Office upheld a CVAC mRNA patent after it was challenged by rival BioNTech , co said on Thursday

** A German court will decide on July 1 if BioNTech infringed this patent

** The dispute is part of a larger ongoing patent battle between CureVac and BioNTech over mRNA tech used in COVID-19 vaccines

** CVAC fell 8.2% in the last 12 months

data.title: Singapore Shares End Week in Red Despite Crossing 4,000 Mark For the First Time
data.description:
Despite The Straits Times Index crossing the 4,000 mark for the first time, Singapore shares slipped into the negative territory for the first time this week, in line with losses across Asia as concerns over US tariffs linger.

The Straits Times Index (STI), a key benchmark for the Singapore Exchange, ranged between 3,969.01 and 4,005.18 throughout the day. It ended the session at 3,972.43, down 9.14 points or 0.23% compared to Thursday's close.

In economic news, Singapore's Import Price Index fell 3.3% year on year in February, extending the 2.2% decrease in the preceding month, according to a report by the Department of Statistics.

Singapore's Manufactured Products Price Index fell 0.5% in February, reversing a 3.6% gain in January, the Department of Statistics reported. Singapore's Domestic Supply Price Index also fell 0.7% in February after a 3.6% rise in January

Meanwhile, total loans and advances to residents in Singapore totaled SG$841.1 billion in February, with building and construction loans contributing SG$177.8 billion, according to data released by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

In company news, shares of Singapore Post were up over 5% after the company completed the divestment of its Australian logistics business, Freight Management to Pacific Equity Partners, for AU$1.02 billion.

Wilmar International's shares were up nearly 1% after the company's near and long term emissions reduction targets were validated by the Science Based Targets initiative.

Meanwhile, shares of Singapore Telecommunications were up nearly 1% at the close after its technology services arm, NCS, entered into a joint venture with Philippine-based Globe Telecom .

data.title: Kosovo's jobless rate flat y/y at 10.7% in Q2 ‘24
data.description:
PRISTINA (Kosovo), March 28 (SeeNews) - Kosovo's unemployment rate stood at 10.7% in the second quarter of 2024, unchanged from the same period of 2023, the statistical agency, ASK, said.

During the April-June period of 2024, the unemployment rate was 16.8% among women and 8.1% among men, ASK said in a quarterly labour force survey on Thursday.

The jobless rate in the 15-24 years age group rose to 21.2% in the review period, from 16.2% a year earlier.

ASK has said that delays in the publication of employment figures are due to a staff shortage.

Kosovo has a population of around 1.59 million, ASK said in July last year.

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • none

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • No

data.title: Barclays, Kepler up Ferrari after 20% drop from peak
data.description:
** Barclays upgrades Ferrari to "overweight" from "equal weight", citing the Italian luxury sports carmaker's relative "safe-haven status" amid uncertainty in the auto sector

** Ferrari shares rise as much as 3.4%

** Barclays cites the company's commercial policy update, which confirmed 2025 targets, despite the latest U.S tariffs on auto imports

** Ferrari's recent 20% fall from its peak last February offers a good entry point, the broker says

** Similarly, Kepler Chevreux upgrades Ferrari to "buy" from "hold", pointing to the recent sell-off after the Exor placing

** "This is the pit stop we were long awaiting to turn more positive", says Kepler

** Out of 16 analysts covering the stock, nine rate it "strong buy" or "buy", six rate it "hold" and one rates it "sell"

data.title: Carnegie Access: Maha Energy: Interview – A chat with the new CEO about the change in strategy
data.description:
Equity analyst Markus Almerud interviews Maha Energy's new CEO Roberto Marchiori. They discuss the profile of Maha after the change of strategy, what new investments they will be looking for, their thoughts about the future of Brava, Maha's valuation discount to the value of the Brava position and the recent developments in Venezuela.

Find the interview on Carnegie Play: https://youtu.be/eo16IdDlPGw

This is commissioned research from Carnegie Investment Bank. Read more here: https://www.carnegie.se/cr/

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Spot Prices Decline in Week Ended March 26, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. natural gas spot prices fell at most locations in the week ended March 26, with the benchmark Henry Hub spot price dropping US$0.36 to US$3.86/million British thermal units, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its Natural Gas Weekly Update released on Thursday.

U.S. natural gas supply edged higher by 0.1%, driven by a sharp rise in average net imports from Canada, the EIA said, citing data from S&P Global Commodity Insights.

U.S. natural gas consumption increased by 4.1%, with residential and commercial sector use surging over the week, the EIA reported.

Net injections into natural gas storage amounted to 37 billion cubic feet for the week ended March 21, resulting in total working gas stocks of 1,744 Bcf, the agency said. This was 122 Bcf lower than the five year average and 557 Bcf lower than the year-ago period.

data.title: Relais Group Plc has agreed on a new long-term financing agreement
data.description:
Relais Group Oyj

Relais Group Plc

Stock Exchange Release 28 March 2025 at 11:45 a.m. EET

Relais Group Plc has signed a EUR 140 million long-term financing agreement with its main bank. The financing agreement amends and extends the Facilities agreement originally concluded in 2019 and previously amended in April 2024. The new financing agreement has a maturity of three years, with two one-year extension options. The maturity date of the previous amendment agreement was 31 May 2026.

According to the new agreement the maximum financial exposure is EUR 140 million consisting of EUR 110 million multi-currency term loan, a Revolving Credit Facility “(RCF”) of EUR 10 million, and an uncommitted term loan facility of EUR 20 million. The new facilities will be used for refinancing the company’s existing debt, financing of future acquisitions as well as for general corporate purposes.

“The new financing package supports the implementation of our growth strategy in an excellent way. Corporate acquisitions, along with organic growth, are key parts of our strategy and we aim to continue being an active player in the consolidation of the vehicle aftermarket”, says Arni Ekholm, CEO of Relais Group.

Relais Group Plc 

Board of Directors 

Further information: 

Arni Ekholm, CEO 

Phone: +358 40 760 3323 

E-mail: arni.ekholm@relais.fi

Distribution:

Nasdaq Helsinki

Key Media

www.relais.fi

Relais Group

Relais Group is a leading consolidator and acquisition platform on the vehicle aftermarket in the Nordic and Baltic countries. We have a sector focus in vehicle life cycle enhancement and related services. We also serve as a growth platform for the companies we own.

We are a profitable company seeking strong growth. We carry out targeted acquisitions in line with our growth strategy and want to be an active player in the consolidation of the aftermarket in our area of operation. Our acquisitions are targeted at companies having a good strategic fit with our group companies.

Our net sales in 2024 was EUR 322.6 (2023: 284.3) million. During 2024, we completed two acquisitions. We employ approximately 1,300 professionals in six different countries. The Relais Group share is listed on the Main Market of Nasdaq Helsinki with the stock symbol RELAIS.

www.relais.fi

data.title: India pushes to ease international payments through homegrown network to rival Visa, Mastercard
data.description:
India is asking the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), a global money-laundering watchdog, to reduce compliance requirements for cross-border payments made through its homegrown system, several sources familiar with the discussions said.

Launched in 2016, the Unified Payment Interface (UPI) accounted for 83% of India's digital payments volume in 2024, up from 34% in 2019, and dominates the domestic retail payments sector.

The government now wants to increase the use of its own payments network by Indians travelling abroad, which could potentially tap into a growing market, and make the global cross-border payments market more competitive.

But its expansion for cross-border payments has been hampered by compliance requirements on smaller payments, to which payments made via networks such as Visa , Mastercard , and SWIFT are not subject, the sources said.

Government officials raised the issue at an FATF conference in Mumbai this week, two of the sources said, declining to be named because they are not authorised to speak to media.

The FATF, India's central bank and the federal finance ministry did not immediately respond to emails seeking comment.

Visa and Mastercard did not respond to requests for comment.

A final decision would depend on achieving a consensus among FATF member countries after the public consultation period, a third source familiar with the discussions said.

A public consultation on the FATF's "travel rule", which requires financial institutions to collect, hold, and transmit information about the sender and receiver of cross-border payments, is open until April 18.

In their current form, global anti-money laundering rules tend to favour both card networks and payments facilitated by the SWIFT payment system, the three sources said.

Central Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra told the FATF gathering that "it would be desirable to make the (FATF's) travel rule technology-neutral," without specific mention of UPI.

India has so far inked deals with seven nations, including France and Singapore, which allow merchants to accept payments via the UPI.

($1 = 85.6175 Indian rupees)
